title: TCP Three-Way Handshake Simulator
description: A transport-layer training tool simulating the RFC 793 TCP three-way handshake with client/server state machines, rAF-animated packet capsules (SYN/SYN-ACK/ACK) moving across a canvas bandwidth pipe, stochastic packet drop fault injection with timeout overlay, and a Wireshark-style segment ledger.
author: Girish Madarkar
features: RFC 793 state machine: Client (CLOSED → SYN_SENT → ESTABLISHED) and Server (LISTEN → SYN_RECEIVED → ESTABLISHED) with randomized ISN (x=1000-8999, y=5000-9999). States update via DOM elements with color-coded text

4-phase handshake pipeline: async/await initiateHandshake() sequences SYN (client→server, seq=x, ack=0), SYN-ACK (server→client, seq=y, ack=x+1), ACK (client→server, seq=x+1, ack=y+1), then both ESTABLISHED

Canvas packet animation: roundRect capsules with shadowBlur=10 glow, lerping from clientX to serverX (0.018 speed). Each packet displays its flag name ([SYN] cyan, [SYN-ACK] magenta, [ACK] green). Bandwidth pipe rendered as dashed line between nodes

Drop fault injection: toggle button sets dropEnabled flag. On next handshake initiation, injectDrop() dissolves active packet midway (dissolved=true, renders as 6 random crimson particle dots), halts handshakePhase, shows modal timeout overlay with 'Connection Timeout: Packet Dropped'
Retransmit: clicking RETRANSMIT clears packets, resets handshakePhase to 0, calls initiateHandshake() to restart from SYN. Disabled when no timeout active

Wireshark-style ledger: addLedger() appends rows to scrolling table with columns SRC, DST, FLAGS (color-coded: SYN=cyan, SYN-ACK=magenta, ACK=green, DROP=amber), SEQ, ACK, WIN. Each row scrolls into view on append

Sequence math: client.seq starts at ISN x, server.seq at ISN y. After SYN: server.ack = client.seq + 1. After SYN-ACK: client.ack = server.seq + 1. After ACK: both ack values validated. Displayed live in 'SEQ MAP' telemetry as 'x=N y=N'
Telemetry panel: Client State, Server State, Active Sequence Matrix (x/y), Last Transmitted Segment Type — all updated synchronously with phase transitions
